meta.label: juggle_chain1
meta.note: FIRST LINK OF THE HARD RULE SATISFIED. Gate 1: ball bounced over the lintel at t=0.79s, drone threaded THAT SAME gate's window at t=1.10s - lag 0.31s. Under the hard rule (credit = unbroken gate-1-anchored run of bounce+window pairs) this scores CHAIN=1. policy_v230 (raw 25) scores CHAIN=0: it never threads window 1 at all, and laps the other windows 2.8-6.9s after the ball is dead. HONEST LIMITS: only 1 of 4 gates, the drone CRASHES after threading, plant score is 0.087. This is the correct motion, not yet a working policy.

meta.label: juggle_pairs_rally
meta.note: SUPERSEDED / DOES NOT SATISFY THE HARD RULE. This run pairs gate 2 (bounce t=2.01 -> window t=2.66, lag 0.65s) but NEVER threads gate 1's window, so under the hard rule (credit is a strict gate-1-anchored chain of bounce+window pairs) it scores CHAIN=0. Kept for contrast only. It is still strictly better-behaved than policy_v230 (raw 25), which flings the ball over all 4 gates, lets it settle at 3.61s, then flies BACKWARDS through the windows at 4.87/6.67/7.88 - that also scores CHAIN=0.

meta.label: juggle_p2_rally
meta.note: Phase-2 milestone: first bg=4 gate rally (all 4 lintels bounced, ball to x=12.4). Drone tips over post-rally (known: fitness nocrash=0), windows 1/4, no target settle. Nominal specialist (suite raw 1.82); Phase-3 multi-case robustness search now running.
